D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

RGB Laser Projection

three monochromatic lasers give an enormous colour gamut — but their coherence sprinkles the image with shimmering speckle

A laser projector combines red, green, and blue lasers. Because each is nearly a single wavelength, the primaries sit almost on the spectral locus, covering a gamut far beyond sRGB. The price is coherence: light scattered from a rough screen interferes with itself, producing a grainy "speckle" pattern. Despeckling breaks the coherence by vibrating a diffuser or summing many states.

Raise the despeckle strength and the shimmer averages away. Switch to the gamut view to see how laser primaries cover far more of Rec. 2020 than broad LED phosphors.
